message:
refactor(web): extract FrameworkWebRuntime to encapsulate fastapi server internals

Moves the fastapi server lifecycle (startup hooks, listener binding,
task spawning, drain, shutdown hooks) from web/server.rs into a
FrameworkWebRuntime struct in web_framework.rs, so the outer web module
no longer carries transport/runtime implementation details.

FrameworkWebRuntime provides:
- start(): runs startup hooks, binds listener, spawns serve task
- signal_shutdown(): triggers graceful shutdown
- join_handle_mut(): access to the join handle for select! patterns
- finish(): handles join result, drains connections, runs shutdown hooks

WebServerHandle simplified:
- Now holds only bound_addr + FrameworkWebRuntime (was server + app + join)
- shutdown() delegates to runtime.signal_shutdown() + runtime.finish()

server.rs cleanup:
- start_web_server() calls FrameworkWebRuntime::start() instead of
  manually constructing server/listener/handler/task
- run_web_server() uses runtime.join_handle_mut() and runtime.finish()
- Removed handle_server_exit() — logic moved into FrameworkWebRuntime::finish()

web.rs import cleanup:
- Removed unused Handler, ServerConfig, ServerError, StartupOutcome, TcpServer
- Added FrameworkWebRuntime import
- Removed unused TcpListener import (now used inside framework)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
